mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 1 month

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate reflects tight labor market conditions that may signal inflationary pressures, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in 1 month

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window for INDPRO's relationship with SPX returns

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and residential construction spending signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST trends over 2-year rolling windows

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPI) combined with low monetary policy rates creates a 'stagflation' risk environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to eroding real returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 5% YoY and FEDFUNDS < 4%

mechanism_narrative: Growing residential mortgage credit availability (RSAFS) indicates expanding housing market demand, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 for RSAFS's relationship with SPX returns over 1-year rolling windows

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust employment conditions that may drive consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized recovery in GDP and housing starts suggests broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST trends over 2-year rolling windows
